note: Sprint 5.28 Trinity Task Queue Dashboard v0.1: adds scripts/trinity/task_queue_dashboard.py as the read-only pane of glass over the autonomy loop built across Sprints 5.23-5.27. The Dashboard scans queue.json + per-status item files + per-item operator_run.json + watchdog reports + per-batch runner reports and emits a deterministic dashboard JSON (schemas/trinity/task_queue_dashboard.schema.json, $id=trinity-task-queue-dashboard/v0.1) plus a static HTML view. No new autonomy — only visibility into the existing one. Dashboard JSON fields: schema, dashboard_id (^dsh-[0-9a-f]{16}$), pinned_time, queue_dir_basename, queue_id, counts (pending/running/completed/failed/batches), latest_items (queue_item_id, status, updated_at, attempt_count, operator_run_path_basename, watchdog_report_path_basename, governor_decisions_count, watchdog_safety_status), latest_batches (batch_id, attempted_count, completed_count, failed_count, safety_status), warnings, safety_status. Three safety_status values with strict precedence: failed (any per-item watchdog_safety_status=critical OR any per-batch safety_status=failed) > warning (any queue item with status=failed, OR any per-batch safety_status=warning, OR any per-item watchdog_safety_status in {warning, stale}, OR any malformed input) > ok. Static HTML view is intentionally conservative: no JavaScript, no external assets (zero https://, zero http://, zero CDN), tiny inline
